commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds qwen3.6-flash and qwen3.6-max-preview to the Qwen catalog, marks qwen3.6-plus reasoning-capable, and adds Standard-only Coding Plan filtering and manifest suppressions.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review gap: PR head adds the new model rows and suppressions while current docs and focused tests still only cover qwen3.6-plus. I did not run tests because this review was required to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can update Qwen docs and endpoint-catalog coverage without changing the core provider boundary or requiring a product decision.

target.title: feat(qwen): add qwen3.6-flash and qwen3.6-max-preview to model catalog
target.body:
﻿## Summary

- **What changed**: Adds `qwen3.6-flash` and `qwen3.6-max-preview` to the bundled Qwen provider catalog, corrects the `reasoning` flag on `qwen3.6-plus` (was `false`; live API returns `reasoning_content`), and extends the existing Coding Plan suppression mechanism to cover the two new Standard-only model IDs.
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: new model ID constants (`QWEN_36_FLASH_MODEL_ID`, `QWEN_36_MAX_PREVIEW_MODEL_ID`), new `QWEN_STANDARD_ONLY_MODEL_IDS` array, two new catalog rows, `reasoning: true` on `qwen3.6-plus`, updated `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` to filter all three Standard-only IDs.
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: four new `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries (qwen + modelstudio x flash + max-preview) so the two new models are suppressed for Coding Plan users through the manifest path, matching the existing `qwen3.6-plus` suppression pattern.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: `normalizeConfig` retains the existing `qwen3.6-plus`-only filter for explicit user-configured model rows (deliberately not extended to flash/max-preview -- the manifest suppression covers the built-in catalog path; explicit opt-ins are preserved per provider-config contract).
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-flash` and `qwen3.6-max-preview` are now generally available on DashScope Standard endpoints. Without catalog entries, users cannot select them in OpenClaw. The `reasoning: false` flag on `qwen3.6-plus` causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that actively returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: The compaction path, auth flow, Coding Plan routing logic for existing models, and `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` semantics for Coding Plan base URLs are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-flash` and `qwen3.6-max-preview` are not in the OpenClaw Qwen catalog; `qwen3.6-plus` shows `reasoning: false` despite the model returning `reasoning_content`. After this patch all three models are reachable and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope Standard CN endpoint (`https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a single-turn completion request to each of the three model IDs against the DashScope Standard CN endpoint.
  2. Inspected the response body for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` to confirm thinking-mode support.
  3. Confirmed Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`) rejects `qwen3.6-flash` and `qwen3.6-max-preview` as expected (Standard-only).
- **Observed result**: All three models accepted requests and returned `reasoning_content`; `reasoning_tokens` present in `usage`. Coding Plan rejection confirmed for the two new IDs.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live DashScope Standard CN responses -- all three model IDs</summary>

**qwen3.6-plus** (reasoning flag corrected: false -> true)

Request:
```json
{"model":"qwen3.6-plus","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],"max_tokens":50}
```

Response:
```json
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"Four","reasoning_content":"<thinking>2+2=4, answer in one word: Four</thinking>","role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":"stop","index":0}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":22,"completion_tokens":227,"total_tokens":249,"completion_tokens_details":{"reasoning_tokens":221,"text_tokens":227}},"model":"qwen3.6-plus"}
```

**qwen3.6-flash** (new)

Request:
```json
{"model":"qwen3.6-flash","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],"max_tokens":50}
```

Response:
```json
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"Four","reasoning_content":"<thinking>...</thinking>","role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":"stop","index":0}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":22,"completion_tokens":188,"total_tokens":210,"completion_tokens_details":{"reasoning_tokens":182,"text_tokens":188}},"model":"qwen3.6-flash"}
```

**qwen3.6-max-preview** (new)

Request:
```json
{"model":"qwen3.6-max-preview","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],"max_tokens":50}
```

Response:
```json
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"Four","reasoning_content":"<thinking>...</thinking>","role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":"stop","index":0}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":22,"completion_tokens":120,"total_tokens":142,"completion_tokens_details":{"reasoning_tokens":115,"text_tokens":120}},"model":"qwen3.6-max-preview"}
```

All three model IDs accepted by DashScope Standard CN. All three return `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens`, confirming thinking-mode support.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Pure catalog metadata addition. No new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope Standard CN (`https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Standard API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-flash"` to Standard CN endpoint.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.
3. Repeat for `qwen3.6-max-preview` and `qwen3.6-plus`.
4. Confirm `qwen3.6-flash` and `qwen3.6-max-preview` are rejected by Coding Plan endpoint.

### Expected

All three models respond on Standard CN with `reasoning_content` present. Flash and max-preview unavailable on Coding Plan.

### Actual

Matches expected on all four checks.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-flash` live on Standard CN; `qwen3.6-max-preview` live on Standard CN; `qwen3.6-plus` `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected); Coding Plan rejection for new IDs.
- **Edge cases checked**: Coding Plan endpoint behavior for the two new Standard-only IDs.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global/Intl Standard endpoint (`dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com`) -- assumed to mirror CN endpoint model availability per Alibaba Cloud docs.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` -- additive catalog entries; no existing model IDs, endpoints, or auth flows changed.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- Coding Plan users: flash and max-preview are suppressed via manifest `modelCatalog.suppressions` and will not appear in the catalog for Coding Plan configurations. Explicit user opt-ins are preserved (`normalizeConfig` does not strip them).

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-flash` or `qwen3.6-max-preview` become unavailable or change API behavior on Standard endpoints.
  - Mitigation: catalog entries can be removed or adjusted in a follow-up; no runtime dependency introduced.
- **Risk**: Coding Plan suppression does not fire for edge-case base URL variants (trailing slash, uppercase hostname).
  - Mitigation: `isQwenCodingPlanBaseUrl` normalizes via `URL.hostname.toLowerCase().replace(/\\.+$/, "")` before comparing; covered.
target.author: wanybug-com
